original_hypothesis.title: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer Modifier Uses Wrong Boolean Logic, Bricking Vault Administration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses OR (||) in the revert condition: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`. This means it only passes when the caller is BOTH the vault maintainer AND holds the EMERGENCY_ROLE. In contrast, the onlyVaultGoverner modifier correctly uses AND (&&). The same bug exists in onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock. Functions gated by these modifiers (addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, addTrustedPosition) become inaccessible to their intended callers unless they hold both roles simultaneously.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrable logic error that makes critical admin functions unreachable in normal operation, effectively bricking vault configuration. The maintainer address (a timelock contract) cannot add/remove connectors or update trusted tokens. Comparing to onlyVaultGoverner which uses && confirms the bug pattern.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false) - uses || instead of &&; compare to onlyVaultGoverner at lines 47-50 which correctly uses &&

preconditions: Vault must be created with different maintainer and emergency addresses (normal operation)
Caller must be the vault maintainer but NOT also hold EMERGENCY_ROLE

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: OR logic in revert condition requires BOTH conditions to pass instead of EITHER
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: onlyVaultMaintainer: `msg.sender != maintainer || !hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE)` reverts unless both are true; should be `msg.sender != maintainer && !hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE)`
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No tests appear to verify that maintainer alone (without EMERGENCY_ROLE) can call gated functions

analysis_questions: Are there any deployment scripts that grant EMERGENCY_ROLE to the maintainer address?
Can the protocol function at all with this bug given the deployment configuration?
Does the onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock have the identical bug?
validation_requirements: Call addConnector as vault maintainer without EMERGENCY_ROLE - should revert
Call addConnector as EMERGENCY_ROLE holder without being maintainer - should also revert
Compare behavior with onlyVaultGoverner which uses correct logic

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: No attacker needed - protocol is self-bricked. The vault maintainer (intended to be a timelock) cannot manage connectors, trusted tokens, or trusted positions unless it also holds EMERGENCY_ROLE. This prevents normal vault administration.
prerequisites: Vault deployed with separate maintainer and emergency addresses (standard deployment)

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete inability to manage vault connectors and positions; new integrations impossible, compromised connectors cannot be removed
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All vault depositors - vault becomes unmanageable
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires redeployment of Registry contract or granting EMERGENCY_ROLE to maintainer (workaround)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for maintainer-only access (without emergency role) to gated functions

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Easily verifiable by calling any gated function as maintainer

validation_result.mechanism: Wrong boolean operator in access control modifier
validation_result.impact_summary: The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses || (OR) in its revert condition instead of && (AND), requiring callers to be BOTH the vault maintainer AND hold EMERGENCY_ROLE. Functions addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, and updateConnectorTrustedTokens become inaccessible to their intended callers unless they hold both roles simultaneously.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier at Registry.sol lines 34-38 uses `||` in the revert condition: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`. For the function to NOT revert (i.e., for the condition to be false), BOTH sub-conditions must be false: `msg.sender == maintainer` AND `hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == true`. This means only addresses that are both the vault maintainer AND hold EMERGENCY_ROLE can pass. In contrast, `onlyVaultGoverner` at lines 47-50 correctly uses `&&`: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].governer && hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`, which correctly allows either the governer OR an emergency role holder. Similarly, `onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock` at lines 40-44 also correctly uses `&&`. The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier is the only one with the wrong operator.

Note: The hypothesis claimed onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock has the same bug - this is INCORRECT. That modifier correctly uses `&&`. However, the core claim about onlyVaultMaintainer is fully confirmed.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code inspection confirms the bug. Registry.sol line 35 uses `||` where `&&` is required. The pattern mismatch with the other two modifiers (lines 41 and 48) which correctly use `&&` further confirms this is unintentional.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Uses || (OR) in revert condition. For the modifier to pass (not revert), both conditions must be false: msg.sender must equal maintainer AND msg.sender must have EMERGENCY_ROLE. Should use && to allow EITHER the maintainer OR an emergency role holder.
This modifier correctly uses && (AND) in the revert condition, allowing EITHER the governer OR an emergency role holder. This is the correct pattern that onlyVaultMaintainer should follow.
This modifier correctly uses && (AND). Contrary to the hypothesis claim, this modifier does NOT have the same bug. Only onlyVaultMaintainer is affected.

calling_sequence: 1. External caller calls addConnector(vaultId, connectorAddresses, enableds)
3. Condition: msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false
4. If caller is maintainer but NOT emergency role holder: first condition is false, second is true -> true || true = true -> REVERTS
5. If caller has emergency role but is NOT maintainer: first condition is true -> true || anything = true -> REVERTS
6. Only if caller is BOTH maintainer AND has emergency role: false || false = false -> passes
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: Any call to addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, or updateConnectorTrustedTokens by an address that is ONLY the vault maintainer (without also holding EMERGENCY_ROLE) will revert. The maintainer role is intended to be a timelock contract which normally would NOT also hold the EMERGENCY_ROLE. The correct behavior (as implemented in onlyVaultGoverner at line 48) should allow either the vault maintainer or the emergency role holder to pass.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Whether maintainer and emergency are the same address in deployment
config_validation.deployment_evidence: In deploySharedContracts.ts, the registry is deployed with ownerAddress for all three roles (governer, maintainer, emergency). In updateRegistry.ts, addVault is called with '0x8E95f959f1Bd3C4A3Be2bda6089155012fF1a37b' for all addresses. Additionally, updateRole() grants EMERGENCY_ROLE to the same address. This means in the current deployment ALL roles are the same address, which acts as a workaround for the bug. However, the code defect exists regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: No external attacker is needed. The protocol is self-bricked for any deployment where the vault maintainer address does not also hold the EMERGENCY_ROLE. In the provided deployment scripts, the same address is used for all roles, which masks the bug. But the intended architecture (maintainer = timelock, emergency = separate EOA/multisig) would result in the maintainer being unable to call addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, or updateConnectorTrustedTokens. This is a code defect in the access control logic.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL severity for any deployment with role separation. The vault maintainer (intended to be a timelock) cannot: (1) add or enable connectors via addConnector, (2) remove trusted positions via removeTrustedPosition, (3) update connector trusted tokens via updateConnectorTrustedTokens. The only workaround is granting EMERGENCY_ROLE to the maintainer address, which defeats the purpose of role separation. Note: addTrustedPosition uses onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock which is NOT affected by this bug.
recommended_mitigations: Change line 35 from `||` to `&&`: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer && hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`
Add unit tests verifying that the vault maintainer alone (without EMERGENCY_ROLE) can call addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, and updateConnectorTrustedTokens
Add unit tests verifying that an EMERGENCY_ROLE holder alone (without being the maintainer) can also call these functions
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PositionRegistry with separate governer, maintainer, and emergency addresses. 2. Grant MAINTAINER_ROLE to maintainer. Grant EMERGENCY_ROLE to emergency. 3. Call addVault setting vault.maintainer to maintainerAddress. 4. As maintainer (without EMERGENCY_ROLE), call addConnector -> expect revert. 5. Grant EMERGENCY_ROLE to maintainer, call addConnector -> expect success. 6. Compare with onlyVaultGoverner: as governer (without EMERGENCY_ROLE), call changeVaultAddresses -> expect success.
uncertainties: The hypothesis incorrectly claims onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock has the same bug - it does NOT (uses && correctly at line 41). Only onlyVaultMaintainer is affected.

The hypothesis lists addTrustedPosition as affected, but it uses onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock which is correct. Only addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, and updateConnectorTrustedTokens are affected.
Current deployment scripts use the same address for all roles, which masks the bug in practice. Production deployments with proper role separation would expose the issue.
additional_testing_needed: Foundry test: call addConnector as vault maintainer without EMERGENCY_ROLE - verify it reverts
Foundry test: call addConnector as EMERGENCY_ROLE holder without being vault maintainer - verify it also reverts
Foundry test: call changeVaultAddresses (uses correct onlyVaultGoverner) as governer without EMERGENCY_ROLE - verify it succeeds
Verify no other modifiers in the codebase have this same pattern error
